tldr: Headlines across four of five coverage zones uniformly announce Argentina's comeback victory over England as a national-identity triumph and title-defence narrative; French-language outlets alone add an experiential register — fan grief and spectacle-replay — while Le Parisien contributes two titles wholly unrelated to the match.

frames.sub_frame: National title defence; nations to final

evidence.quote: Argentina complete comeback to beat England and reach final
l'Argentine renverse l'Angleterre et défendra son titre
Argentina beat England to reach World Cup final
منتخب الأرجنتين يلحق بإسبانيا إلى الدور النهائي
l'amertume des supporters anglais après la défaite
revivez le succès fou de l'Argentine face à l'Angleterre

single_outlet_findings.finding: Both Le Parisien titles in this cluster ('Sauvons la forêt française' and an ant-invasion/heatwave piece) are entirely unrelated to the World Cup semi-final, suggesting a feed-aggregation or clustering artefact; neither is assignable to any match frame.

bottom_line: The headline layer is a unified result bulletin — Argentina's stoppage-time comeback beats England, Spain final set — with French outlets alone surfacing the emotional aftermath for fans, and Le Parisien's two off-topic titles the sole clustering anomaly.
